title: Getting Started with Next.js
content:
### Creating a Next.js App

The quickest way to start is with `create-next-app`:

```bash
npx create-next-app@latest my-app
cd my-app
npm run dev
```

You will be prompted to configure TypeScript, ESLint, Tailwind CSS, and more.

### Project Structure

A fresh Next.js project has this structure:

```
my-app/
  app/          # App Router (Next.js 13+)
  pages/        # Pages Router (traditional)
  public/       # Static assets (images, fonts)
  styles/       # CSS files
  next.config.js
  package.json
```

The `public/` folder serves static files at the root URL. Place `public/logo.png` and access it at `/logo.png`.

### Package Scripts

Your `package.json` includes these scripts:

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",        // Start development server on port 3000
    "build": "next build",     // Build for production
    "start": "next start",     // Start production server
    "lint": "next lint"        // Run ESLint
  }
}
```

- `npm run dev` — starts the dev server with Fast Refresh
- `npm run build` — creates an optimized production build
- `npm run start` — runs the production server after building

### Running the Development Server

Navigate to `http://localhost:3000`. Edit any file and see instant updates via Fast Refresh. The dev server automatically compiles your code, bundles it, and refreshes the browser.

quiz.question: What command starts the Next.js development server?
Where should static assets like images be placed?
